feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
feed.subtitle: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: UAE FAF FILE FORMAT
entry.content: Why SAP DRC UAE is generating the FAF in TXT format instead of CSV which is required by FTA?

entry.title: SAP DRC VAT REPORTING
entry.content:
SAP DRC UAE VAT "Tax Summary Analytics"

- why the transaction amounts in DRC 'Tax Summary Analytics' not matching with Manage tax items.

- why the DRC 'Tax Summary Analytics' not retrieving all document types as appearing in the Manage tax items.

entry.title: Upload rebate credit memo to KSEF (Polish National System of e-Invoices)
entry.content:
Dear All, I have question about passing rebate credit memos (bonus invoices) to KSEF from ECC.

Our company is using condition based rebate agreement scenario when we pay bonus to customers with quarterly rebate credit memo (via rebate settlement).

From document flow perspective credit memo request is a standalone one - without any reference to F2 invoices which accrued the rebate in the past quarter - so there is no direct link between original invoice and (bonus) credit memo.

If I understand well KSEF requirement is to display every reference invoices (or evenitems?) on the credit memo. But standard ECC Rebate Settlement process (when we credit 100+ invoices, 1000+ items in one shot) is not designed to support this.

Is there a SAP solution or workaround which makes rebate settlement process aligned with KSEF in ECC?

entry.title: E-invoicing France : field to store electronic address in BP
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Where values from Routing Code and Suffix/Addressing Line Code are stored in France eDocuments to S/4HANA Public Cloud?

How can we fulfill this tags and configure it accordingly?

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Inbound France eInvoice via DRC/PEPPOL not arriving in ERP
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am working on a France eInvoicing implementation in a R3-system using SAP DRC with PEPPOL for outbound and inbound invoices. We set up a test environment, in which a customer has the same identification numbers as our seinding system. Using this method in other implementations in the same system, we could send an eInvoice and receive it as well.

Current situation:

  • Outbound invoices (France) are successfully generated in the eDocument Cockpit.
  • In the BTP PEPPOL Exchange, the invoice is visible for our Participant
  • The Participant shows Send and Receive activated in the PEPPOL Exchange UI. -> Sending invoices works as intended
  • In the PEPPOL exchange app in "Monitor documents" it says "Document pushed" regarding a receiving document
  • No inbound eInvoice arrives in our ERP (eDocument Cockpit remains empty)

The question is: Are there special France-specific requirements for incoming invoices? We checked the implemented customizing and master data, but haven't found anything suspicious. The configuration took place according to the sap documentation.

Any hints, configuration tips, or known issues about how inbound eInvoices should be set up for France would be highly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: EDO_IT_INVOICE_V2 not available in english
entry.content:
I see that EDO_IT_INVOICE_V2 is being used for displaying PDF (EDOC_COCKPIT) for italian invoices. When I look at this form in SFP, I don't see any data.  When I logon using italian language, it displays the layout. 

 

Funnily enough, the display PDF functionality works in english. I am trying to copy this form and when I copy the english version, I get the blank screen as well. I could not see any note for this form in SAP. So wondering how to proceed.

entry.title: Seeking Configuration Points for Colombia DRC (F350, Income Tax, and VAT) - S/4HANA Cloud Public Edi
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently working on a localized implementation for Colombia using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.

While navigating the Central Business Configuration (CBC) and Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC), I have successfully identified the configuration points and SSCUIs required to link the "Información Exógena" formats (DIAN 1001 to 1009).

However, I am having trouble locating the specific configuration nodes or SSCUIs for the following statutory reports:

  1. Formato 350 (Monthly Withholding Tax Declaration).

  2. Income Tax Declaration (Declaración de Renta).

  3. VAT Declaration (IVA - Formulario 300).

I have already explored the Tax Box Framework (SSCUIs 103011, 103012, and 103013), but it is not clear if these specific forms follow the same mapping logic as the Exógena formats or if they require a different setup within the "Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting" (102220) activity for this specific release.

Could anyone point me toward the correct configuration path or provide the specific SSCUI IDs for these forms in the 2602 version?

Thanks in advance for your help!

Best regards,

Juan Sebastian Valencia SAP Consultant | Structum Ingeniería S.A.

entry.title: VAT Return Statement Great Britain - Submission to HMRC failed
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

We are using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 2602 having 2SL - Quality and Production tenants.

While submitting Great Britain VAT Report Statement to HMRC through App - Run Statutory Report, we are getting error  "Submission to Authority failed - Refresh Token"

We know that after every 18 months, we have to Grant authority for HMRC. As per SAP help document, system should automatically redirect us to HMRC to grant authority, but this is not happening in our sesyem.

We checked KBA-3094866 3094866 - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance service - Refresh Token Invalid - SAP for Me but that was not helpful, because we are not able to find the solution steps provided in this KBA. we are not able to find "Configure Report Communication screen" mentioned in this KBA.

Kindly provide me the steps to complete this activity to refresh token again.

Thank you.

Regards,

Shrinivas

entry.title: Peppol Exchange - Push the response to the Souce system delays
entry.content:
Good afternoon. We are currently in the process to test the newly implemented DRC from SAP ERP 740 using Cloud Connector to BTP Cloud and we are having issues to receive the results after the invoice is sent to Peppol Exchange. We are seeing delays over 2 days and it has been difficulty to troubleshoot the errors. I followed the steps listed on this link : https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-edition/sap-document-and-reporting-compliance-cloud-edition/integrating-peppol-exchange-with-sap-erp and checked all settings with company assisting us to implement DRC for Belgium. Please let me know if there is any blog related to this issue or SAP notes to check. I already reviewed these notes and did not resolve this issue: 

2926868 - eDocument Peppol Troubleshooting Guide, 3498170 - Troubleshooting guide: Electronic communication integration of SAP Document 

Any assistance or guidance is really appreciated.

entry.title: ZUGFERD vs XRECHNUNG-B2B Customer Invoices from External System
entry.content:
Hi,

In Germany, we can exchange B2B invoices with customers in two ways.

1. via PEPPOL Network (supported via XRECHNUNG profile supported via UBL 2.1)

2. via Email (supported via ZUGFERD supported by CII)

We may have customers who wants invoice in PDF embedded with XML and other may want Invoice to be send over Peppol as pure XML XRechnung invoice. For our client 99 percent of customer invoices are generated in a non-SAP systems.

Now for a customer who opts for Peppol exchange and an invoice is created in external system and the middleware transform it to UBL 2.1 format invoice to be consumed in SAP DRC and SAP DRC creates an eDocument for it so that it can be send over Peppol to the customer, this invoice exchange happens using pure XML Rechnung profile supported by UBL 2.1 format.

Question: For a customer who wishes to have invoice via email method ,PDF (embedded with XML) ZUGFERD profile , will the middleware have to transform the invoice from non-SAP system to CII format rather than UBL 2.1 and then send it to SAP DRC to create an eDocument and then send it to the customer via eDoc_cockpit OR SAP DRC will use UBL 2.1 format to generate an XML which will be consumed by SAP DRC and then SAP convert into a format which is compatible with ZUGFERD (CII) so that it can be send over to customer via Email.

entry.title: Include email-address of buyer contact in electronic customer invoice Germany
entry.content:
Hey everybody,

I'm currently struggeling with some requirements of our customers to include the email of their contact person in e-invoices. Regarding to the xrechnung specification it should be in segment 'BT58'.

Do you guys have a hint for me, how this could be implemented? 

Kind Regards
Marcel

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

entry.title: KSEF - EDOC_COCKPIT - EDOCUMENT099 - EDOC_INTERFACE_CONNECTOR error.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

we are currently trying to implement KSeF in DRC. Our system is S/4HANA Public Cloud and DRC Public Edition. At the moment, we are dealing with the following problem. We cannot submit the invoice or display it, as shown in the screenshots below. When we try to submit it, we get a briefly described error "Check the eDocument application log", and we cannot find details on what might be wrong.

When we try to display document , we get more another error - "eDocument cannot be displayed; check implementation of BAdI EDOC_INTERFACE_CONNECTOR method DISPLAY_EDOCUMENT" , but all notes and advice from the official SAP pages concern ERP and not Public Cloud. Has anyone dealt with this problem, or can someone point out where we should look for the cause of the issue?

entry.title: Storage of e-invoicing identifiers for SAP DRC implementation - EDOEUBUPAV vs Master Data
entry.content:
Hello 

We are now implementing SAP DRC and our SAP system is ERP 6.0. Our implementation partner tells us that to store e-invoicing identifiers we must use external (not linked with master data) table EDOEUBUPAV and using master data for this purpose is "reserved" for SAP HANA only. Can that be true? I can see in customizing section where I can assign Partner ID type to the country specific identifier and then manage the IDs exclusively in BP master data:

And then using new types for storing Peppol (and others depending on country) directly in BP master (Identification numbers section).

Would you please advise if this is really impossible for SAP ERP linked with DRC?

Thanks

Robert

entry.title: DRC - Peppol - Monitor Log - Email notifications for FAILED documents on Peppol Exchange
entry.content:
When we receive documents in Peppol exchange, the documents can sometimes result in Error and dont reach the EDOC_COCKPIT.

Currently, there is no SAP functionality that can notify users when a document fails. Users have to monitor the log manually to keep an eye on possible issues, which would become a problem as increasing number of countries are moving to e-invoicing and can generate huge volume of documents.

Did anyone find a work around for this?

I have also raised a request on CIP. You could vote through the below link to request SAP to add the functionality 

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence  - Request ID: 369471

entry.title: [South Korea] DRC Activation and License Usage
entry.content:
Currently, the system is designed to allow activation by country. Since we have two legal entities in Korea, the system is flagging this as excessive license usage (over-usage).

To resolve this, we intend to activate and use DRC for only one of the two entities. However, we are unsure of the specific steps to configure this at the entity level rather than the country level.

Could you please provide guidance or documentation on how to limit the activation to a single legal entity?

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: DRC Restriction – Support Document (CS) with Inventory Accounts in Public Edition 2602 (Colombia)
entry.content:
Hello,

The current behavior for Support Document Colombia Localization is as follows:

  • The CS document is processed correctly when postings are made against expense accounts.
  • However, a limitation occurs when a supplier not required to issue invoices is involved in transactions related to inventory (raw materials).

Specific scenario:

In cases where there are price differences between the purchase order and the final value of the support document, adjustments are required that impact inventory accounts.

Under this scenario, when sending the document to DIAN via Document Compliance & Reporting (DRC), an error is triggered indicating that document type CS does not allow postings to material/inventory accounts.

Requirement:

We need to enable the use of standard document types KR (Vendor Invoice) or KG (Vendor Credit Memo) for Support Documents, and ensure they are accepted by DRC for submission to DIAN.

Currently, when using KR or KG, the document is posted correctly in the ERP; however, the submission to DIAN fails due to a validation error in DRC.

We would appreciate your guidance on whether there are any SAP-recommended alternatives to handle this type of scenario (inventory adjustments within support documents).

Error message using documents types  KG or KG.

entry.title: DRC VAT Reporting: Supporting Documents/Attachments in USt-VA Submission
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I have a question regarding the submission of VAT Advance Returns (USt-VA) via Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud.

Is it possible to include attachments (e.g., additional documents or explanations) directly from the system when submitting the VAT advance return electronically?
The background is that supporting documents should be sent along with the submission so that the tax authority does not need to request them separately. I would like to carry out the entire process in the Run Statutory Reports app.

If so:

  • Which configuration or process is required to enable this?
  • Are there any limitations depending on the country or interface (e.g., ELSTER)?
  • Is this supported as standard, or does it require an enhancement?

Thank you in advance for your insights and experience.

Best regards,
Vanessa

entry.title: DRC (S/4HANA Private Cloud): ZM Line Items Not Visible in “Run Statutory Reports” Preview
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

we are using Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud to submit statutory reports to the tax authorities.

In this context, the EC Sales List (ZM report) is generated using the Run Statutory Reports app. However, the ZM line items are not displayed in the preview. Interestingly, this issue does not occur in the Public Cloud.

What could be the reason for this, and what needs to be adjusted so that the items are visible in the preview?

The data itself is included in the XML file that is sent to the tax authorities.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Vanessa

#SAP #S4HANA #DRC #TaxReporting #VAT #ECsaleslist #PrivateCloud #RunStatutoryReports #SAPCommunity #ELSTER

entry.title: SAP Note 3413099 – DDIC activation errors – DRC France (SAP ECC6 EHP6)
entry.content:
Hello,

I’m currently implementing SAP DRC for France and installing SAP Note 3413099.

During implementation, I get DDIC activation errors.

From the SNOTE log (attached), main issues are:

  • Tables EDOFRSIACTNCODE and EDOFRSICODMOTIF are not active

  • System indicates they “must be created in the database”

  • Dependent objects (search helps, table types) fail due to these tables

  • Example from log:
    “Table EDOFRSIACTNCODE must be created in the database”
    “EDOFRSIACTNCODE is not a database table => not suitable as selection method”

Has anyone already faced this issue?
What would be the recommended way to resolve it?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Greek DRC - movement 261
entry.content:
Dear all,

We are setting up the DRC for Greece and have successfully installed all OSS notes listed in the master note 2930400, and have managed to customize the movement types 101, 301, 601 and 641. We have sent the information to myData and printed the QR.

However, we can't make movement 261 work; we have assigned the edocument type GR_DELN, the invoice type 10.1, movement of purpose "Other transfers" (value 19) and even tried with "Intra-company transfers" (value 😎 in table edogrinvtypbwart, but any time we print it in the J2GLPPMM tcode we get an error, and when checked in the edoc_cockpit it says "Delivery address cannot be determined", even with all the addresses in the master data are maintained.

Could you share any ideas to fix it?
